title: API Keys Are Like Credit Cards
subtitle: API keys, seed phrases, and wallet security. What access to give your agent. What to never give.

sections.heading: The Horror Stories Are Real
sections.content: Leaked API keys. Drained wallets. Exposed credentials on GitHub. These aren't hypothetical — they happen daily.

sections.heading: What API Keys Actually Are
sections.content: An API key is a password to a paid service. Anyone with the key can run up your bill. Treat it like a credit card number.

sections.heading: Seed Phrases & Web3 Wallets
sections.content: Seed phrases = master password to your crypto. Never give these to any agent. Ever. No exceptions.

sections.heading: The Access Levels
sections.content: Read-only: safe. Write: careful. Admin: dangerous. Financial: never without builder approval for each action.

sections.heading: The Checklist
sections.content: Use .env files. Add .env to .gitignore. Rotate keys regularly. Set spending limits. Monitor usage daily.

sections.heading: Skill Safety
sections.content: Before installing agent skills/tools: check permissions, read code, sandbox test, verify developer.

sections.heading: What to Give, What to Keep
sections.content: Give: API keys with spending limits, read access to code, sandboxed environments. Keep: seed phrases, admin passwords, production credentials.

sections.heading: The 5-Minute Security Setup
sections.content: Create .env. Add to .gitignore. Set API spending limits. Enable 2FA everywhere. Done.

key_takeaways: Treat API keys like credit cards — leaked keys mean real financial damage
Never give seed phrases or admin passwords to any agent, ever
The 5-minute setup: .env file, .gitignore, spending limits, 2FA everywhere
